pi_items.source: Task description and environment setup scripts (install_gretl.sh, task_utils.sh) confirm food.gdt is the dataset used
Setup and export scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh) verify this is the expected output location
OLS regression computed from actual food.gdt data: food_exp = const + slope * income, where const is the intercept coefficient
OLS regression computed from actual food.gdt data: food_exp = const + slope * income, where slope is the income coefficient
OLS regression R-squared computed from actual food.gdt data: R² = 1 - (SS_residual / SS_total)
Explicitly specified in task.json as the observation number for the structural break test. No contradictory evidence found.
install_gretl.sh script lines 146-166 specify these URLs as sources for POE5 data

pi_items.verified_value: Hill, Griffiths and Lim, Principles of Econometrics, 5th ed., Table 2.1. Real household survey data: food_exp = weekly food expenditure in dollars, income = weekly income in $100 units.
pi_items.source: install_gretl.sh script lines 189-193 document the data source

privileged_info_summary: The food.gdt dataset is real household survey data from Hill, Griffiths and Lim, Principles of Econometrics, 5th Edition, Table 2.1, containing 40 observations. The OLS regression of food_exp on income produces: constant=79.886, slope=12.315, R²=0.881. The metadata claims constant=83.416, slope=10.210, R²=0.385, all of which are contradicted by the actual data. The claimed constant differs by 3.530 (exceeds tolerance of 1.0), the slope differs by 2.105 (exceeds tolerance of 1.0), and R² differs by 0.496 (exceeds tolerance of 0.05). The Chow test observation point at 20 is verified as specified in the task.
